_note: v2 adds a 'description' field to every vocabulary entry. The 'values' array preserves the v1 enum list for validators. 'described' maps each value to {label, description} for UI and documentation.

protein_domain.label: Protein Domain
protein_domain.description: A low-level protein part or sequence-defined actuator used as a building block inside larger engineered compositions.

multi_component_switch.label: Multi-Component Switch
multi_component_switch.description: An architecture-level composition assembled from multiple interacting parts to produce stimulus-responsive behavior.

rna_element.label: RNA Element
rna_element.description: A low-level RNA regulatory part, such as a riboswitch, aptamer, or ribozyme, used inside larger engineered compositions.

construct_pattern.label: Construct Pattern
construct_pattern.description: An architecture-level pattern for arranging parts into a reusable engineered composition.

engineering_method.label: Engineering Method
engineering_method.description: A method-type item describing an experimental practice for building, optimizing, or evolving engineered systems.

assay_method.label: Assay Method
assay_method.description: A method-type item describing how engineered systems are measured, benchmarked, or characterized.

computation_method.label: Computational Method
computation_method.description: A method-type item describing a computational approach for designing, ranking, or analyzing engineered systems.

delivery_harness.label: Delivery Strategy
delivery_harness.description: An architecture-level delivery strategy describing how an engineered system is packaged, delivered, and made operational in a target context.

composition_hierarchy._note: Mechanism branch for the thing being engineered. Mechanisms are the top conceptual layer; architectures are composed arrangements that realize or deploy mechanisms; components are the lowest-level parts or sequence-defined elements.

mechanism.label: Mechanism
mechanism.description: A biophysical mode of action such as heterodimerization, photocleavage, RNA binding, or translation control.

architecture.label: Architecture
architecture.description: A composed arrangement that realizes one or more mechanisms, such as a multi-component switch or reusable construct pattern.

component.label: Component
component.description: A low-level part or sequence-defined element, such as a protein domain or RNA element, used within an architecture.

technique_hierarchy._note: Separate hierarchy for methods of use. Techniques describe high-level engineering practices or approaches; methods are concrete methods, assays, or computational tools that instantiate those activities.

technique.label: Approach
technique.description: A high-level engineering practice or approach, such as computational design, sequence verification, or functional assay.

method.label: Method
method.description: A concrete method-type item used to design, build, verify, or characterize an engineered system.

item_type_taxonomy._note: Maps each item_type to the branch it primarily belongs to. Workflows combine the mechanism branch with the technique branch.

delivery_harness.label: Delivery Strategy
delivery_harness.description: Deployment architecture for getting a system into context.

seed.label: Seed
seed.description: Initial dossier created from known references. Citations, validation data, and scores are placeholders awaiting source-backed curation.

normalized.label: Normalized
normalized.description: Data ingested and normalized from external sources, but not yet reviewed by a human curator.

curated.label: Curated
curated.description: Fully reviewed: citations verified, validation observations confirmed, scores computed from evidence.

deprecated.label: Deprecated
deprecated.description: No longer recommended: superseded by a better tool, retracted, or found to be unreliable.

research.label: Research
research.description: Early-stage: demonstrated in academic research settings only.

preclinical.label: Preclinical
preclinical.description: Validated in animal models or advanced in vitro systems with translational intent.

clinical.label: Clinical
clinical.description: In human clinical trials or with clinical-grade manufacturing evidence.

deployed.label: Deployed
deployed.description: Commercially available, FDA-approved, or in routine production use.

review.label: Review
review.description: A review or meta-analysis that surveys the field rather than presenting original data.

primary_paper.label: Primary Paper
primary_paper.description: An original research article presenting new experimental results.

trial_record.label: Trial Record
trial_record.description: A clinical trial registration or results record (e.g. ClinicalTrials.gov).

database_entry.label: Database Entry
database_entry.description: A structured record from a curated database (e.g. UniProt, Addgene, PDB).

protocol.label: Protocol
protocol.description: A detailed laboratory protocol or methods paper.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.description: A systematic comparison study benchmarking multiple tools or methods.

preprint.label: Preprint
preprint.description: A manuscript posted to a preprint server (e.g. bioRxiv) before peer review.

supports.label: Supports
supports.description: The evidence supports the claim or validates the tool's function as described.

contradicts.label: Contradicts
contradicts.description: The evidence contradicts the claim—the tool did not perform as expected.

mixed.label: Mixed
mixed.description: Results are ambiguous: partial support or context-dependent outcomes.

neutral.label: Neutral
neutral.description: The source mentions the tool but does not provide evidence for or against.

foundational.label: Foundational
foundational.description: The original paper that first described or created this tool.

best_review.label: Best Review
best_review.description: The most comprehensive review covering this tool's mechanism, applications, and limitations.

independent_validation.label: Independent Validation
independent_validation.description: A study from an independent lab that reproduced or validated the tool's function.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.description: A head-to-head comparison of this tool against alternatives in the same category.

protocol.label: Protocol
protocol.description: A detailed methods paper or protocol for using this tool in the lab.

therapeutic.label: Therapeutic
therapeutic.description: Evidence of this tool's use in a therapeutic context: gene therapy, drug development, or clinical intervention.

negative_result.label: Negative Result
negative_result.description: A study reporting failure, limitations, or unexpected problems with this tool.

structural.label: Structural
structural.description: Structural biology data (crystal structure, cryo-EM) informing how this tool works at atomic resolution.

database_reference.label: Database Reference
database_reference.description: A curated database record (e.g. Addgene plasmid, PDB entry) for this tool.

cell_free.label: Cell-free
cell_free.description: Cell-free systems: purified protein, in vitro transcription/translation, or lysate-based assays.

bacteria.label: Bacteria
bacteria.description: Bacterial hosts such as E. coli, demonstrating function in a prokaryotic context.

yeast.label: Yeast
yeast.description: Yeast systems (S. cerevisiae, S. pombe), bridging prokaryotic simplicity and eukaryotic biology.

mammalian_cell_line.label: Mammalian Cell Line
mammalian_cell_line.description: Immortalized mammalian cell lines (e.g. HEK293, HeLa, CHO) showing function in eukaryotic cells.

primary_cells.label: Primary Cells
primary_cells.description: Primary cells isolated directly from tissue, providing more physiologically relevant context than cell lines.

organoid.label: Organoid
organoid.description: 3D organoid cultures that recapitulate tissue architecture and cell-type diversity.

mouse.label: Mouse
mouse.description: Mouse models in vivo, demonstrating function in a living mammalian organism.

large_animal.label: Large Animal
large_animal.description: Large animal models (e.g. non-human primate, pig) providing translational data closer to human physiology.

human_clinical.label: Human Clinical
human_clinical.description: Human clinical trials or clinical-grade applications in patients.

mechanistic_demo.label: Mechanistic Demo
mechanistic_demo.description: An experiment demonstrating the tool's core mechanism of action (e.g. light-induced dimerization confirmed by co-IP).

application_demo.label: Application Demo
application_demo.description: A downstream application using this tool to achieve a biological goal (e.g. optogenetic gene activation in neurons).

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.description: A quantitative comparison against alternative tools measuring the same output.

therapeutic_use.label: Therapeutic Use
therapeutic_use.description: Use in a therapeutic or clinical context: gene therapy, drug development, or patient treatment.

manufacturing_use.label: Manufacturing Use
manufacturing_use.description: Use in biomanufacturing, industrial fermentation, or production-scale applications.

failed_attempt.label: Failed Attempt
failed_attempt.description: A documented failure: the tool did not work as expected in this context. Valuable negative data.

success.label: Success
success.description: The tool performed as expected with clear positive results.

mixed.label: Mixed
mixed.description: Partial success: some metrics met expectations while others fell short, or results were context-dependent.

failed.label: Failed
failed.description: The tool did not achieve the intended outcome in this experiment.

fast_screen.label: Fast Screen
fast_screen.description: A rapid workflow for testing a small number of candidates with minimal cloning (days to one week).

standard_construct.label: Standard Construct
standard_construct.description: A typical construct-build-test cycle: design, clone, verify, express, assay (one to three weeks).

library_selection.label: Library Selection
library_selection.description: A multi-round selection workflow starting from a molecular library (e.g. phage display, FACS sorting).

in_vivo_pilot.label: In Vivo Pilot
in_vivo_pilot.description: An in vivo experiment requiring packaging, delivery, and animal work (weeks to months).

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: A user-defined workflow that does not fit standard templates.

design.label: Design
design.description: Computational or manual design of the candidate sequence, construct, or library.

dna_acquisition.label: DNA Acquisition
dna_acquisition.description: Ordering or synthesizing DNA: gene fragments, oligos, or full-length genes from a vendor.

assembly.label: Assembly
assembly.description: Molecular cloning or assembly of DNA parts into a functional construct (e.g. Gibson, Golden Gate).

transformation.label: Transformation
transformation.description: Introducing DNA into bacterial cells for amplification or screening.

colony_screen.label: Colony Screen
colony_screen.description: Screening bacterial colonies to identify correct clones after transformation.

sequence_verification.label: Sequence Verification
sequence_verification.description: Sanger or NGS sequencing to confirm the construct matches the intended design.

transfection.label: Transfection
transfection.description: Introducing DNA/RNA into mammalian or eukaryotic cells for expression.

expression.label: Expression
expression.description: Growing cells and expressing the construct to produce functional protein.

selection_round.label: Selection Round
selection_round.description: One round of selection or enrichment from a molecular library (e.g. phage display, FACS).

assay.label: Assay
assay.description: Functional measurement: fluorescence, binding, activity, or phenotypic readout.

analysis.label: Analysis
analysis.description: Data analysis and interpretation of experimental results.

decision.label: Decision
decision.description: Go/no-go decision point based on accumulated data.

packaging.label: Packaging
packaging.description: Packaging DNA/RNA into delivery vehicles (e.g. AAV, LNP).

delivery.label: Delivery
delivery.description: Delivering the packaged construct to target cells or tissues in vivo.

fold_induction.label: Fold Induction
fold_induction.description: Ratio of output signal in the ON state vs OFF state. Higher is better.

repression_efficiency.label: Repression Efficiency
repression_efficiency.description: Fraction of target expression that is silenced (0–1). Higher means more complete repression.

dark_state_leak.label: Dark-State Leak
dark_state_leak.description: Residual activity in the OFF/unstimulated state. Lower is better for tight control.

on_kinetics_minutes.label: ON Kinetics (min)
on_kinetics_minutes.description: Time to reach half-maximal activation after stimulus onset. Faster enables tighter temporal control.

off_kinetics_minutes.label: OFF Kinetics (min)
off_kinetics_minutes.description: Time to return to half-maximal after stimulus removal. Faster enables reversible control.

affinity_kd.label: Affinity (Kd)
affinity_kd.description: Dissociation constant for the binding interaction. Lower Kd means tighter binding.

viability_percent.label: Viability (%)
viability_percent.description: Percentage of cells surviving after tool expression or treatment. Higher is less toxic.

toxicity_signal.label: Toxicity Signal
toxicity_signal.description: Qualitative or quantitative indicator of cytotoxicity from the tool or its stimulus.

editing_efficiency_percent.label: Editing Efficiency (%)
editing_efficiency_percent.description: Fraction of target loci successfully edited. Higher means more on-target activity.

localization_precision.label: Localization Precision
localization_precision.description: Spatial resolution of subcellular targeting (e.g. nuclear vs cytoplasmic enrichment ratio).

signal_to_noise.label: Signal-to-Noise
signal_to_noise.description: Ratio of specific signal to background noise. Higher indicates cleaner readout.

tissue_penetration_compatibility.label: Tissue Penetration Compatibility
tissue_penetration_compatibility.description: Qualitative rating of whether the tool's stimulus can reach deep tissues in vivo.

heterodimerization.label: Heterodimerization
heterodimerization.description: Two different proteins are brought together by a stimulus, enabling recruitment or complex formation.

oligomerization.label: Oligomerization
oligomerization.description: A protein self-associates into multimers upon stimulation, enabling clustering or activation.

conformational_uncaging.label: Conformational Uncaging
conformational_uncaging.description: A stimulus triggers a structural change that exposes a previously hidden functional element.

membrane_recruitment.label: Membrane Recruitment
membrane_recruitment.description: A protein is recruited to a membrane surface (e.g. plasma membrane) by a stimulus.

photocleavage.label: Photocleavage
photocleavage.description: Light breaks a covalent bond, irreversibly releasing a caged peptide or domain.

dna_binding.label: DNA Binding
dna_binding.description: A protein binds DNA in a stimulus-dependent manner to regulate gene expression.

rna_binding.label: RNA Binding
rna_binding.description: A protein or RNA element binds RNA to control translation or stability.

degradation.label: Degradation
degradation.description: A stimulus triggers targeted degradation of a protein via the proteasome or other pathways.

translation_control.label: Translation Control
translation_control.description: Post-transcriptional regulation of mRNA translation rate or efficiency.

computational_design.label: Computational Design
computational_design.description: In silico design of protein sequences, structures, or circuits using algorithms or machine learning.

selection_enrichment.label: Selection / Enrichment
selection_enrichment.description: Experimental selection from a library to enrich for variants with desired properties.

directed_evolution.label: Directed Evolution
directed_evolution.description: Iterative mutagenesis and selection to evolve improved biological parts.

sequence_verification.label: Sequence Verification
sequence_verification.description: Confirming construct identity by Sanger or next-generation sequencing.

functional_assay.label: Functional Assay
functional_assay.description: Measuring the activity or performance of an engineered part in a biological context.

structural_characterization.label: Structural Characterization
structural_characterization.description: Determining 3D structure via X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, or NMR.

transcription.label: Transcription
transcription.description: Regulation of gene transcription (activation, repression, or modification of mRNA synthesis).

translation.label: Translation
translation.description: Control of mRNA translation into protein.

localization.label: Localization
localization.description: Control of subcellular localization (e.g. nuclear import/export, membrane targeting).

degradation.label: Degradation
degradation.description: Targeted degradation of proteins or RNA molecules.

signaling.label: Signaling
signaling.description: Activation or modulation of intracellular signaling pathways.

editing.label: Editing
editing.description: Genome or epigenome editing at specific loci.

selection.label: Selection
selection.description: Enrichment or screening of variants from a molecular library.

diagnostic.label: Diagnostic
diagnostic.description: Diagnostic or biosensing applications for detecting analytes or biomarkers.

modalities._note: Not present in v1. Added in v2 to canonicalize the input/output modality vocabulary.

light.label: Light
light.description: Controlled by light wavelength and intensity (optogenetic).

chemical.label: Chemical
chemical.description: Controlled by small molecules, drugs, or chemical inducers.

mechanical.label: Mechanical
mechanical.description: Controlled by physical force or mechanical stress.

sequence.label: Sequence
sequence.description: Takes a DNA/RNA/protein sequence as input.

structure.label: Structure
structure.description: Takes a 3D molecular structure as input.

conformational_change.label: Conformational Change
conformational_change.description: Produces a structural/conformational change in a protein or domain.

localization.label: Localization
localization.description: Controls subcellular localization (e.g. nuclear, membrane, cytoplasmic).

degradation.label: Degradation
degradation.description: Triggers targeted protein or RNA degradation.

signaling.label: Signaling
signaling.description: Activates or modulates a cell signaling pathway.

selection.label: Selection
selection.description: Enriches or selects variants from a library.

analysis.label: Analysis
analysis.description: Produces a computational analysis or prediction.

score_definitions._note: Defines the four computed scores in replication_summary. Not an enum vocabulary but included here as canonical definitions for UI display.

evidence_strength_score.label: Evidence
evidence_strength_score.description: Strength of the published evidence base: paper count, citation quality, and breadth of experimental support.

replication_score.label: Replication
replication_score.description: Degree of independent reproduction: how many distinct labs, institutions, and biological contexts have confirmed the finding.

practicality_score.label: Practicality
practicality_score.description: Ease of real-world use: accounts for known failure modes, setup complexity, cost, and documented pitfalls.

translatability_score.label: Translatability
translatability_score.description: Path toward therapeutic or deployed use: regulatory precedent, in vivo data, clinical trials, and manufacturing readiness.

validation_rollup_fields._note: Maps the boolean rollup fields to the biological_system_levels they summarize, plus two non-system-level flags.

has_cell_free_validation.label: Cell-free
has_cell_free_validation.description: At least one successful observation in a cell-free system.

has_bacterial_validation.label: Bacteria
has_bacterial_validation.description: At least one successful observation in bacterial hosts.

has_mammalian_cell_validation.label: Mammalian
has_mammalian_cell_validation.description: At least one successful observation in mammalian cell lines.

has_mouse_in_vivo_validation.label: Mouse
has_mouse_in_vivo_validation.description: At least one successful observation in mouse models in vivo.

has_human_clinical_validation.label: Human
has_human_clinical_validation.description: Evidence from human clinical trials or clinical-grade applications.

has_therapeutic_use.label: Therapeutic
has_therapeutic_use.description: Used in a therapeutic context: drug development, gene therapy, or clinical intervention.

has_independent_replication.label: Indep. Replication
has_independent_replication.description: Independently replicated by a lab unaffiliated with the original authors.

mechanistic_match_score.label: Mechanistic Match
mechanistic_match_score.description: How well the tool's mechanism aligns with the problem's biological requirements.

context_match_score.label: Context Match
context_match_score.description: Whether the tool has been validated in the relevant biological context (organism, cell type, tissue).

throughput_match_score.label: Throughput Match
throughput_match_score.description: Whether the tool's throughput matches the scale needed for the problem.

time_to_first_test_score.label: Time to First Test
time_to_first_test_score.description: Estimated calendar time from decision to first experimental result.

cost_to_first_test_score.label: Cost to First Test
cost_to_first_test_score.description: Estimated direct cost (reagents + services) to reach first experimental result.

replication_confidence_modifier.label: Replication Confidence
replication_confidence_modifier.description: Adjustment based on how well the tool has been independently replicated.

practicality_modifier.label: Practicality
practicality_modifier.description: Adjustment for known failure modes, setup complexity, and documented pitfalls.

translatability_modifier.label: Translatability
translatability_modifier.description: Adjustment for regulatory precedent, clinical data, and manufacturing readiness.

overall_gap_applicability_score.label: Overall Applicability
overall_gap_applicability_score.description: Composite score representing how well this tool fits the stated problem.
